title: Math/stats consolidation + DSA Sliding Window finish
focus: Recap weeks 1-2 with rehearsed answers. Wrap Sliding Window pattern.

items.label: Bias-variance decomposition with worked example
interviewQuestions: Decompose expected squared error into bias², variance, and irreducible noise.
Why does adding more training data reduce variance but not bias?

items.label: Double descent: when more parameters help
interviewQuestions: Explain the double-descent phenomenon. How does it overturn classical bias-variance intuition?
Why do over-parameterized models often generalize well in deep learning?

items.label: Learning curves: diagnose underfit vs overfit
interviewQuestions: How do you read a learning curve to decide between more data, regularization, or a bigger model?
What does a large gap between training and validation curves usually mean — and what shrinks it?

items.label: Convex vs non-convex optimization
interviewQuestions: What does convexity guarantee for optimization?
Are deep neural network losses convex? Why does SGD still work?

items.label: Gradient descent, SGD, mini-batch SGD
interviewQuestions: Compare batch GD, SGD, and mini-batch SGD — trade-offs in compute, noise, and convergence.
Why does SGD with momentum converge faster than vanilla SGD?

items.label: Code gradient descent for a scalar loss
interviewQuestions: Implement gradient descent for a simple squared-error objective and explain the update rule line by line.
How would you debug a training run where gradient descent diverges after a few steps?

items.label: Momentum, Nesterov, Adam, AdamW, schedulers
interviewQuestions: Compare Adam, AdamW, and SGD with momentum — which would you reach for first and why?
Why is the AdamW correction important when using weight decay with adaptive optimizers?
What's the role of learning-rate warmup and cosine schedules?
Decompose expected squared error into bias², variance, and irreducible noise — give an example of each.
How would you diagnose whether to add more data, regularize harder, or grow the model from a learning curve?
Explain double descent — why does it overturn the classical bias-variance picture?
